title: Warhammer Quest - Take a Look - Part 12 - The White Dwarf Issues (d)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TEqu2JH5dYc

Daniel (@dungeondive) continues through the later White Dwarf issues where Warhammer Quest content was thinning as Games Workshop shifted focus toward Necromunda and 40K.

Issue 196 features "On the Waterfront" — the first of a two-part series introducing coastal towns, ocean journeys, and the Lost Kingdoms. It adds a harbour with new locations (Trading Post, the Brig, smuggling opportunities), a full harbour events table rolled on d66, and a waterfront tavern with its own encounter chart. Issue 197 completes the series with rules for hiring ship captains via a d6 table — from weather cancellations to passage aboard Admiral Krueger's war galleon — plus ocean event tables for the perilous months at sea and the reward of richer treasure in the Lost Kingdoms.

Issue 200, the biggest White Dwarf ever, disappointingly offers only rules for converting Warhammer Quest warriors to Fantasy Battle. Issue 201 delivers "The Green and Pestilent Land," a Nurgle-themed mini expansion Daniel suspects was another scrapped boxed set. It includes a Chaos Sorcerer of Nurgle, plague room monster tables featuring plague bearers, Skaven plague monks, and giant rats, plus chaos sorcery spells like Cloud of Flies and Stream of Corruption.

----

Would you set sail for the Lost Kingdoms, or stay safely in the dungeons beneath the Old World?
